source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A problem of practical interest is to make a beam of electrons turn a \( 90^\circ \) corner. This can be done with the parallel-plate capacitor shown in figure. An electron with kinetic energy \( 3.0 imes 10^{-17}\\,\\mathrm{J} \) enters through a small hole in the bottom plate of the capacitor. What strength electric field is needed if the electron is to emerge from an exit hole \( 1.0\\,\\mathrm{cm} \) away from the entrance hole, traveling at right angles to its original direction?
